yt_search_query: time travel faster than light paradox
text_block: If you could travel faster than light, you would arrive before you left, allowing you to watch yourself <red>leave</red> from the comfort of the <yellow>future</yellow>.
According to quantum physics, particles don't exist in definite locations until measured, meaning <yellow>reality</yellow> is just a cloud of probabilities until you <red>look at it</red>.
Because space is expanding faster than light, the stars we see today will eventually vanish, leaving future civilizations in a <yellow>dark void</yellow> convinced they are <red>alone</red>.
In a quantum switch, it is possible for two events to happen in a state where <yellow>A causes B</yellow> and <red>B causes A</red> at the same time.
